quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz 2, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Classification of Matter: Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Elements, Compounds, Homogeneous Mixtures, Heterogeneous Mixtures, Pure Substances, Colloids, Chemical vs Physical Separation

questions.question: Which classification best describes oxygen gas (O₂)?
questions.explanation: Oxygen gas (O₂) is an element because it consists of only one type of atom. Although it exists as diatomic molecules, it cannot be chemically broken down into simpler substances.

questions.question: Which statement BEST distinguishes a compound from a mixture?
questions.explanation: Compounds are substances where two or more elements are chemically bonded in fixed proportions. Mixtures are not chemically bonded and can be separated by physical means.

questions.question: Salt dissolved completely in water is classified as a:
questions.explanation: When salt dissolves completely in water, it forms a homogeneous mixture (solution) with uniform composition throughout. The particles are evenly distributed and not visible.

questions.question: Which of the following is a heterogeneous mixture?
questions.explanation: Salad is a heterogeneous mixture because its components (lettuce, tomatoes, etc.) are visibly different and not uniformly distributed. Air, vinegar, and brass are homogeneous mixtures/solutions.

questions.question: Which property remains constant for a pure substance?
questions.explanation: A pure substance has a fixed, constant composition (e.g., water always H₂O). Shape, mass, and volume can vary depending on sample size or container.

questions.question: A substance can be separated into simpler substances by chemical means only. It is most likely a:
questions.explanation: Compounds require chemical reactions to break them into simpler substances. Mixtures can be separated physically, and elements cannot be broken down further by chemical means.

questions.question: Which classification applies to a substance with uniform composition throughout?
questions.explanation: A homogeneous mixture has uniform composition throughout, meaning its components are evenly distributed at the molecular level.

questions.question: Which of the following is BOTH a mixture and homogeneous?
questions.explanation: Steel is an alloy—a homogeneous mixture of iron and other elements (like carbon). Table sugar and distilled water are pure compounds; carbon dioxide is a pure compound.

questions.question: Which statement about elements is correct?
options: They contain two or more types of atoms
They are gases at normal room temperature
They can be separated into simpler substances
questions.explanation: Elements are the simplest substances—they cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means. They consist of only one type of atom.

questions.question: Which of the following is a pure substance?
questions.explanation: Gold is an element, making it a pure substance. Milk, air, and soil are all mixtures containing multiple components.

questions.question: Which pair is correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) is a compound—two elements chemically bonded. Salt (NaCl) is also a compound, not an element. Oxygen is an element. Granite is a heterogeneous mixture, not a solution.

questions.question: Which observation BEST indicates a chemical compound?
questions.explanation: Compounds have a fixed ratio of elements by mass (law of definite proportions). Visible components or variable ratios indicate mixtures. Filtration separates mixtures, not compounds.

questions.question: Which classification would apply to air?
questions.explanation: Air is a homogeneous mixture (solution) of gases—primarily nitrogen, oxygen, and small amounts of other gases. Its composition is uniform throughout.

questions.question: Which of the following is a colloid?
questions.explanation: Milk is a colloid—a mixture where particles are dispersed but not dissolved, and do not settle out. Saltwater is a solution, sand in water is a suspension, and sugar crystals are a pure substance.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the classification of matter! 🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between elements, compounds, and mixtures.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on homogeneous vs heterogeneous mixtures.
scoreMessages.0: Please review pure substances, compounds, and how mixtures are classified.
